q: A capacitor is an electrical device that stores electrical energy in the form of an electric field.
A capacitor consists of two conductors separated by a large distance.
The capacitor was originally known as a ___.
Which type of capacitors use plastic film as the dielectric medium?
Film capacitors are available in nearly any value and working voltages up to $1500\ V$.
In which circuits are ceramic capacitors typically used?
a: High-frequency circuits such as audio frequency ($af$) to radio frequency ($rf$)

q: Ceramic capacitors are mainly used for high stability performances with low ___.
The capacitance values of ceramic capacitors are stable with respect to applied voltage, frequency, and temperature.
What material is used as the dielectric in electrolytic capacitors?
The two main types of electrolytic capacitors are ___ and aluminum.
The failure of electrolytic capacitors can be hazardous, resulting in an explosion or fire.
Variable capacitors mostly use what as the dielectric medium?
The capacitance of a variable capacitor can be mechanically adjusted.
A supercapacitor (SC) is also called an ___.
Supercapacitors typically store $10$ to $100$ times more energy than electrolytic capacitors.
Which application uses supercapacitors for power backup?
Film capacitors and ceramic capacitors are polarized capacitors.
Non-polarized capacitors do not have ___ (positive and negative terminals).
What is one primary use of capacitors in power supplies?
Capacitors are used for blocking DC current after getting fully charged but allow the AC current to pass through it.
